dimensions.note: No secrets stored or vaulted. Uses the ambient AWS credential chain via AWS_PROFILE plus a read-only sts:GetCallerIdentity call; expectedAccountId and requiredProfileSuffix are non-secret config. Nothing sensitive is logged (account id and ARN are non-secret identifiers).

info on entry (verifying context) and completion (context verified); debug before the STS call and inside safeDestroy for ignored cleanup failures. Structured placeholders throughout; no secret values logged.

Fails closed: throws on unset/mismatched profile, missing/mismatched account, empty STS Account, and invalid globalArgs. All throws occur before writeResource, so no partial state. The new try/finally around client.send only adds cleanup; safeDestroy swallows its own errors at debug and cannot mask or replace the original send() outcome, so a real STS failure still propagates unchanged.

aws_context_guard_test.ts covers suffix mismatch/match/bypass, account mismatch, full-success writeResource assertion, STS error propagation, unset profile, empty Account, and globalArgs parse failure. New tests assert verify destroys the STS client exactly once on both the success and the STS-error path, and a safeDestroy unit test asserts single invocation, swallowed throwing destroy (logged), and tolerance of undefined/{}/no-logger. STS send and destroy are mocked via prototype; env saved/restored in finally.

Read-only verification with a single writeResource('context','current') keyed by a fixed instance name, so re-runs overwrite rather than duplicate. No created cloud resources to orphan. The STS client's socket pool is now freed on every code path via the finally-block safeDestroy.
Single sts:GetCallerIdentity call; response fields (Account/Arn/UserId) defensively defaulted and validated (empty Account throws). No pagination needed for this API.

The STSClient is now explicitly destroy()-ed in a finally block via the inlined safeDestroy helper, freeing its connection pool on both the success and error paths — matching the sibling @jentz/aws-rds-reservations pattern. safeDestroy is no-throw best-effort cleanup. No file handles or temp files; no created resources to track.
README and tests use placeholder identifiers (123456789012 account id, example ARNs, generic profile names). No real IPs, hostnames, account ids, or topology.

ContextSchema and GlobalArgsSchema are plain z.object with no .passthrough(); expectedAccountId is regex-validated to 12 digits, verifiedAt is z.iso.datetime(). Mechanical schema-write conformance: verify writes accountId/arn/userId/profile/region/verifiedAt, a 1:1 match with ContextSchema's six fields, no placeholders.
The context resource declares lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10, matching the sibling extensions' convention.

Not a CRUD lifecycle model. It is a workflow-safety primitive whose only method (verify) asserts identity and records a derived context snapshot; there is no managed cloud resource to create/update/delete/sync.
verify performs no mutating AWS calls (sts:GetCallerIdentity is read-only), so there is no destructive action that a policy/live preflight label would gate.
Single spec 'context' written with the fixed key 'current' from the single method; no cross-spec or cross-method name collisions possible.
verify never reads back stored state — it derives the context fresh from env + STS each run. No update/delete/sync path that would need readResource.

The model now carries a no-op upgrade chain (2026.06.07.1 -> 2026.06.08.1 -> 2026.06.09.1) ending at the current model.version, mirroring the sibling @jentz/aws-rds-inventory and aws-rds-reservations convention with the Array<{...}> cast for clean deno check. globalArguments (expectedAccountId, requiredProfileSuffix) is unchanged since the 2026.05.17.1 baseline so every upgradeAttributes is identity; each entry still advances the stored typeVersion so any future schema-changing upgrade chains cleanly. The never-published 2026.06.08.2 is intentionally omitted since no stored instance ever carried it. Resolves the version-upgrades follow-up the prior review flagged for this bump.
